rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into implementation scopes when binding implementation-level data flows, not component types.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL scope boundaries: use component type scopes for visible input/output contracts, implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Only reference features explicitly declared in the AADL model—never infer ports or paths not present in the structural extract.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes in implementations must use assign for output bindings, not guarantee or assume.
Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place component-level guarantees in implementation annexes or use subcomponent paths in type-level contracts.
Never encode event ports as Boolean or numeric values without explicit data-value declarations in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
